perturbation.original_text: The Company hereby appoints Deutsche Bank as the initial Remarketing Agent, and Deutsche 3 Bank hereby accepts appointment as Remarketing Agent, for the purpose of (i) Remarketing the Remarketed Securities on behalf of the holders thereof, (ii) establishing the Reset Rates (as defined below) for the Securities in connection with the Remarketing and (iii) performing such other duties as are assigned to the Remarketing Agents in the Remarketing Procedures, all in accordance with and pursuant to the Remarketing Procedures.
perturbation.changed_text: The Company hereby appoints Deutsche Bank as the initial Remarketing Agent, and Deutsche 3 Bank hereby accepts appointment as Remarketing Agent, for the purpose of (i) facilitating the remarketing activities but not directly remarketing the Remarketed Securities on behalf of the holders thereof, (ii) establishing the Reset Rates (as defined below) for the Securities in connection with the Remarketing and (iii) performing such other duties as are assigned to the Remarketing Agents in the Remarketing Procedures, all in accordance with and pursuant to the Remarketing Procedures.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the role of the Remarketing Agent from "Remarketing the Remarketed Securities on behalf of the holders thereof" to "facilitating the remarketing activities but not directly remarketing the Remarketed Securities on behalf of the holders thereof", this introduces an in-text contradiction. It's now unclear whether the Remarketing Agents are actively selling the securities or simply providing support. This contradicts with Section 1(b), which states that the remarketing agents agree 'to use commercially reasonable efforts to remarket the Remarketed Securities tendered or deemed tendered to the Remarketing Agents in the Remarketing'
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) The Remarketing Agents agree (i) to use commercially reasonable efforts to remarket the Remarketed Securities tendered or deemed tendered to the Remarketing Agents in the Remarketing, (ii) to establish the Reset Rates in accordance with the Remarketing Procedures and to notify the Company, the Trustee and the Stock Purchase Contract Agent promptly of the Reset Rates and (iii) to carry out such other duties as are assigned to the Remarketing Agents in the Remarketing Procedures, all in accordance with the provisions of the Remarketing Procedures.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the role of the Remarketing Agent from directly 'Remarketing the Remarketed Securities on behalf of the holders thereof' to 'facilitating the remarketing activities but not directly remarketing the Remarketed Securities on behalf of the holders thereof.' This directly contradicts the contradicted text, Section (b), which states the Remarketing Agents agree 'to use commercially reasonable efforts to remarket the Remarketed Securities tendered or deemed tendered to the Remarketing Agents in the Remarketing.' This is a clear in-text contradiction and ambiguity regarding the agent's responsibilities.

perturbation.original_text: The obligations of the Remarketing Agents shall be subject, in their discretion, to the condition that all representations and warranties and other statements of the Company herein or in certificates of any officer of the Company or any subsidiary of the Company delivered pursuant to the provisions hereof are, at the Applicable Time, the Remarketing Date and the Remarketing Settlement Date (in each case, to the extent such representations and warranties are applicable as of such date), true and correct, the condition that the Company shall have performed all of its covenants and other obligations included hereunder and in the other Transaction Documents to be performed at or before the date hereof, the Applicable Time, the Remarketing Date and the Remarketing Settlement Date, and the following additional conditions:
perturbation.changed_text: The obligations of the Remarketing Agents shall be subject to, without regard to their discretion, to the condition that all representations and warranties and other statements of the Company herein or in certificates of any officer of the Company or any subsidiary of the Company delivered pursuant to the provisions hereof are, at the Applicable Time, the Remarketing Date and the Remarketing Settlement Date (in each case, to the extent such representations and warranties are applicable as of such date), true and correct, the condition that the Company shall have performed all of its covenants and other obligations included hereunder and in the other Transaction Documents to be performed at or before the date hereof, the Applicable Time, the Remarketing Date and the Remarketing Settlement Date, and the following additional conditions:
perturbation.explanation: The removal of the phrase “in their discretion” significantly limits the Remarketing Agent’s ability to subjectively assess whether the conditions have been met. Now it becomes an obligation instead of a discretion, creating an in-text contradiction if unforeseen issues arise or if the Remarketing Agents believe it's imprudent to proceed due to non-material breaches as the word “shall” turns it into a mandatory fulfillment and the phrase “the Remarketing Agents’ obligations under this Remarketing Agreement shall be suspended until such default or event has been cured” in Section 1(k) mentions that, if there’s and event or default under the Indenture, then the obligations can be suspended, which creates a conflict between conditions that need to be fulfilled.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (k) If at any time during the term of this Remarketing Agreement, any Event of Default (as defined in the Indenture) or event that with the passage of time or the giving of notice or both would become such an Event of Default has occurred and is continuing under the Indenture, then the obligations and duties of the Remarketing Agents under this Remarketing Agreement shall be suspended until such default or event has been cured. The Company will promptly cause the Trustee, the Stock Purchase Contract Agent and the Collateral Agent to give the Remarketing Agents notice of all such defaults and events of which such trustee or agent is aware.
perturbation.justification: The removal of "in their discretion" from the original text transforms the Remarketing Agents' ability to assess condition fulfillment from a discretionary power to a mandatory obligation. This directly contradicts section (k), which allows for the suspension of the Remarketing Agents' obligations in the event of a default under the Indenture. By removing the discretion, the modified text creates a scenario where the agents are obligated to proceed even if a default exists, thus contradicting their right to suspend obligations as stated in section (k).

perturbation.original_text: This Remarketing Agreement supersedes all prior agreements and understandings (whether written or oral) between the Company and the Remarketing Agents, or any of them, with respect to the subject matter hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: This Remarketing Agreement supersedes all prior agreements and understandings (whether written or oral) between the Company and the Remarketing Agents, or any of them, with respect to the subject matter hereof, except for any side letters or agreements related to expenses reimbursement.
perturbation.explanation: By adding "except for any side letters or agreements related to expenses reimbursement," a contradiction is created. While Section 4 specifies how fees and expenses are handled, this addition allows for potentially undisclosed expense agreements. This statement is contradicting on the rest of the section. This undermines the purpose of Section 4. Fees and Expenses, where the Company covenants and agrees with the Remarketing Agents that the Company will pay or cause to be paid the expenses that are specified on that section
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. Fees and Expenses. The Company covenants and agrees with the Remarketing Agents that the Company will pay or cause to be paid the following: (i) the fees, disbursements and expenses of counsel and accountants to the Company in connection with the registration of the Securities under the Act and all other expenses in connection with the preparation, printing and filing of the Registration Statement, any Preliminary Prospectus, any Issuer Free Writing Prospectus and the Final Prospectus and any amendments and supplements thereto and the mailing and delivering of copies thereof to the Remarketing Agents and dealers; (ii) the cost of printing or producing the Transaction Documents, any Blue Sky Survey and any other documents in connection with the Remarketing of the Remarketed Securities; (iii) all expenses in connection with the qualification of the Remarketed Securities for offering and sale under state securities laws and insurance securities laws as provided in Section 3(b) hereof, including the reasonable fees and disbursements of counsel for the Remarketing Agents in connection with such qualification and in connection with the Blue Sky Survey; (iv) the filing fees incident to, and the fees and disbursements of counsel for the Remarketing Agents in connection with, securing any required review by the Financial Industry Regulatory 21 Authority of the terms of the sale of the Remarketed Securities; (v) any fees charged by securities rating services for rating the Remarketed Securities; (vi) the cost of preparing the Remarketed Securities; (vii) the fees and expenses of any trustee, paying agent or transfer agent and the fees and disbursements of counsel for any such trustee, paying agent or transfer agent in connection with the Remarketing; (viii) any travel expenses of the Company's officers and employees and any other expenses of the Company in connection with attending or hosting meetings with respect to this Remarketing; and (ix) all other costs and expenses incident to the performance of the obligations of the Company hereunder which are not otherwise specifically provided for in this Section. Except as provided in this Section, and Sections 6 and 13 hereof, the Remarketing Agents will pay all of their own costs and expenses, including the fees of their counsel, stock transfer taxes on resale of any of the Remarketed Securities by them and any advertising expenses connected with Remarketing of the Remarketed Securities that they may make.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an exception to the superseding clause for 'side letters or agreements related to expenses reimbursement.' This contradicts Section 4, which is intended to be the sole source for defining expense responsibilities. By allowing for undisclosed expense agreements outside of Section 4, the modification creates a direct conflict regarding which expenses the company is obligated to pay, leading to potential disputes and uncertainty about the enforceability of Section 4.
